RM input + partial_w + gamma+beta hung. Step 1: verify simpler RM no-affine aligned case works. If it does, suspect partial_w handling or gamma/beta tilize in RM context. If RM no-affine fails, the tilize-input path itself is bad (CB sizing, sticks reader, or asymmetric tilize).

Writer stuck CWFW on cb_output. Reader (NCRISC) and compute (TRISC) idle. Cause: untilize helper ALWAYS outputs tile-sized pages, but my cb_output for RM output has page_size=padded_row_bytes. Mismatch → untilize pushes 1 page of tile_size data, but writer waits for 1 page of padded_row_bytes data (or some other size mismatch). Fix: cb_output uses tile_size pages for both TILE and RM output; writer for RM uses write_sticks_after_untilize helper.

fixes: cb_output page_size is now always tile_size (was padded_row_bytes for RM output)
writer uses write_sticks_after_untilize<cb_output> helper for RM output
reader iterates tr-outer pass-inner to match compute order (was pass-outer tr-inner)

hypothesis: The full Refinement 2 suite (16 test cases) likely hangs on one case — the most-suspect candidates are: (a) wide-W RM input streaming (test_streaming_no_affine[rm-bf16-w4096/8192]) — the per-partial-stick noc_async_read loop, (b) wide-W with gamma/beta (test_streaming_with_gamma_beta) — the per-block gamma tilize might have a CB sync issue if cb_gamma_sticks has 32 pages capacity but we only push 1 — the LLK could read garbage and cause unexpected state. Plan: run cases individually (test_streaming_no_affine first for both layouts and shapes), then with gamma/beta, then multi-core, narrowing down which case hangs.
